name: Khawla bint Hussain
title: Jasmine of Damascus
shortDescription: One of Imam Hussain's (AS) young daughters who passed away in the city of Baalbek on the harsh route of captivity from Kufa to Sham.
fullStory:
Khawla bint Hussain was one of Imam Hussain's (AS) youngest daughters present in the Karbala caravan. Like the other children of the sanctuary, she witnessed the Kufans' countless crimes and her loved ones' martyrdom on Ashura.

After the tents were looted, the young Khawla was taken captive along with the other women and children. The route from Kufa to Sham was one of the harshest historical routes, where captives were taken on bare-backed camels under the scorching sun.

On this long and arduous journey, Khawla became severely ill due to thirst, exhaustion, hunger, and extreme psychological pressure. When the caravan of captives reached the area of 'Baalbek' (in modern-day Lebanon), this little girl could no longer continue.

Khawla passed away unjustly right there in the city of Baalbek and was buried in that same location. Today, her holy shrine in Baalbek, Lebanon, is a place of pilgrimage for the lovers of the Ahl al-Bayt.
quoteArabic: السَّلامُ عَلَيْكِ يا بِنْتَ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ...
quote: (In her eulogy:) Peace be upon you, O beloved of Hussain, whose life was taken by the suffering of captivity.
source: Local sources of Lebanon / History of Baalbek
